The reviewer’s note is correct in general, but my conditioning already treated “at least one die shows a 6” as a constraint on the ordered pair of outcomes.

Condition on the set  
\(S=\{(a,b): a=6 \text{ or } b=6\}\), which has \(6+6-1=11\) outcomes.

Among these, the sum is even exactly when the *other* die is even (since \(6\) is even):  
\((6,2),(6,4),(6,6),(2,6),(4,6)\) → 5 outcomes.

So \(P(\text{even sum}\mid \text{at least one 6}) = 5/11\).

ANSWER: 5/11 | CONFIDENCE: 100
